headline: A wave of dissent sweeps through Servant of the People, Zelensky's party
description: As Ukraine prepares to pass a series of laws in order to secure international funding, the president appears to have lost some of his influence over his parliamentary majority. His loyal supporters are trying to keep his party cohesive, as it's deeply connected to his rise to power.

headline: 'Historic day': Artemis II astronauts travel further into space than ever before
Trump doubles down on Iran threat, says ceasefire 'not good enough'
On immigration, Spain moves against the tide of its European neighbors
description: Citing economic and moral arguments, Spanish Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's Socialist government is preparing to grant legal status to 500,000 undocumented workers, most from Latin America. The plan brings hope to the 840,000 foreign nationals living without papers in Spain.

headline: In Hungary, unease grows over Viktor Orban's crony capitalism
description: Companies that have thrived on public contracts since the Fidesz party came to power in 2010 have seen their share prices tumble ahead of the April 12 legislative elections, as polls indicate that opposition candidate Péter Magyar could win.

headline: Sex is an art form hidden in the shadows
description: Moral panic over the pleasures of the flesh prevents us from recognizing the artistic dimension of sexuality, as well as from passing down the best practices, argues Le Monde columnist  Maïa Mazaurette.

headline: Are Trump's ideologues intellectuals or imposters?
description: Curtis Yarvin has become a leading figure in the Neo-Reactionary movement (also known as the Dark Enlightenment), which has now made it to the White House. But can this blogger really be considered an intellectual? Political theorist Arnaud Miranda and sociologist Olivier Alexandre debate the origins and roles of one of the thinkers who has Trump's ear.

headline: War denies Lebanon's dead a final resting place
Israel renews Lebanon strikes, forcing Syria border crossing to close
Key facts to understand cadmium, an emerging public health issue
Greek PM calls for launch of fraud proceedings in EU agricultural subsidies case
South Korea's president regrets 'reckless' drones sent into the North
Why is Donald Trump so mean?
Netanyahu ousts top aide after racist remarks
Sociologist Dominique Méda on the 'lessons of Lionel Jospin and Jürgen Habermas'
Artemis II astronauts ready for Moon flyby on fifth day of historic mission
Sharks and 50-course meals: Restaurants race to provide 'experiences'
'The power struggle between Saudi Arabia and the UAE is only on hold'
Will Self: 'Our historical condition is a peculiar mixture of omniscience and impotence'
China's execution of a Frenchman convicted of drug trafficking casts a chill on relations with Paris
description: Chan Thao Phoumy was sentenced in 2010 after authorities dismantled a methamphetamine trafficking network. France has condemned the fact that his defense was not allowed to attend his final court hearing.

headline: Le Duc brings the fresh taste of the sea to Paris
description: For nearly 60 years, Le Duc, a family-run Parisian institution known for its delicate fish recipes, has drawn a loyal following who come to savor impossibly thinly sliced, pearly raw sea bass or langoustines with red shiso and wasabi.

headline: How Morocco is renewing its influence networks in France
description: Some of Morocco's traditional networks of influence in France have been put on hold in recent years. How they might be restructured remains uncertain, while new voices have struggled to replace high-profile figures.

headline: 'Can Le Pen and Bardella accept betraying a portion of their own voters to get elected?'
Death penalty for Palestinians accused of 'terrorist' murder continues Israeli government's downward spiral
The EU must respond to the challenges posed by Viktor Orban
